entries.title: Sine, Cosine, Tangent (Right Triangle)
entries.content: Ratios of sides in a right triangle relative to an acute angle. SOH-CAH-TOA mnemonic: sin = opposite/hypotenuse, cos = adjacent/hypotenuse, tan = opposite/adjacent.
entries.formula: sin θ = O/H; cos θ = A/H; tan θ = O/A

entries.title: Pythagorean Identity
entries.content: Fundamental identity relating sine and cosine for any angle θ. Derived from unit circle where x² + y² = 1.

entries.title: Unit Circle Values (30°, 45°, 60°)
entries.content: Common exact values: sin 30° = 1/2, cos 30° = √3/2; sin 45° = cos 45° = √2/2; sin 60° = √3/2, cos 60° = 1/2.

entries.title: Law of Cosines
entries.content: Generalizes the Pythagorean theorem to any triangle. Useful when two sides and included angle are known, or all three sides.

entries.title: Double Angle Formulas
entries.content: Express sin(2θ) and cos(2θ) in terms of sin θ and cos θ. Used in integration and simplifying trigonometric expressions.
